sections.paragraph: (an intensitive form) i.q. יָבֵשׁ, only in fem. יַבָּשָׁה that which is dry, das Trockene. בַּיַּבָּשָׁה on the dry, dry footed, Exo 14:16 22:29 Jos 4:22. Hence used for dry land, as opposed to sea, Gen 1:9 Exo 4:9 Jon 1:9, 13 Jon 1:13 2:11 Psa 66:6. So in Gr. ἡ ξηπά and τὸ ξηρόν, Mat 23:15 (opp. ἡ θάλασσα), Sir_37:3 Vorstius, De Hebraismis N. Test. ed. Fischer, cap. 2, § 2.
